steps.title: Match the underlined claim
steps.content: The underlined claim says Beloved resembles classic magical realist novels because it juxtaposes literary realism with long-established Black American cultural traditions. A supporting quotation therefore needs both parts: realistic fiction and cultural traditions that create an antirealistic or realism-transcending element. A quotation about only influence, alternation, or general tradition would not directly support the claim.

steps.title: Reject distorted support
steps.content: Choice A frames Black American traditions and Latin American magical realism as competing influences, which is not the underlined claim. Choice B says Black American cultural traditions figure prominently in Latin American magical realism, distorting the passage's relationship between those traditions and Beloved. Choice C says Beloved alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes while Black American traditions remain constant, so it does not show the specific juxtaposition.

steps.title: Select direct evidence
steps.content: Choice D says much of Beloved follows realistic fiction while Morrison also includes elements from Black American cultural traditions that transcend realism. This directly places literary realism beside Black American cultural traditions in the way the underlined claim describes. The answer is D.
